title: Enrichment Explanation (themes format)
description: LLM-generated structured explanation of GO enrichment output in depth-anchor themes format. Fill every field strictly from the input enrichment data provided — do not introduce gene symbols or GO IDs that are not present in the input. IMPORTANT: All text fields must contain plain text only — do not use markdown syntax (no **, __, *, #, backticks, or any other markdown formatting). The rendering layer will apply all formatting.

themes.description: One explanation object per theme in the input, in the same order as the input themes array.

theme_index.description: 0-based index matching the position of this theme in the input themes array.

narrative.description: 2-3 sentence biological narrative explaining what this theme means and why these genes cluster together. Plain text only — no markdown formatting. Tag EVERY sentence with exactly one of: [DATA] for statements derived directly from the enrichment numbers, [GO-HIERARCHY] for statements about how GO terms relate to each other, [INFERENCE] for mechanistic or biological interpretations that go beyond the raw numbers, or [EXTERNAL] for claims that require knowledge external to the enrichment data (e.g. known biology from the literature). Use only genes and GO terms from this theme's input data. Do NOT merely restate GO term names — explain the biological mechanism. BAD: 'ABC is involved in regulating receptor signaling pathways.' GOOD: 'ABC antagonises ligand-mediated TGF-beta signaling, limiting downstream Smad2/3 phosphorylation PMID:1269767.'

key_insights.description: 2-4 key biological insights about this theme. Each insight must reference a specific GO term from this theme's input data.

insight.description: One specific biological insight. Plain text only — no markdown formatting. Be concrete — reference gene names or processes from the input data.

go_id.description: The GO term ID most relevant to this insight. MUST be one of the GO IDs present in this theme's input data (anchor or specific terms).

key_genes.description: 2-5 key genes for this theme. You MUST select only from the candidate_key_genes list provided in the input for this theme — do not introduce genes from external knowledge.

gene.description: Gene symbol. MUST appear in the candidate_key_genes list for this theme.

go_id.description: The specific GO term in this theme that most directly involves this gene. MUST be from this theme's input data.

description.description: One sentence explaining this gene's MECHANISTIC role in the context of this theme. Plain text only — no markdown formatting. Do NOT merely restate the GO annotation — explain HOW the gene acts. BAD: 'ABC is involved in cell migration.' GOOD: 'ABC suppresses cell migration via PI3K/AKT-mediated focal adhesion disassembly PMID:19188590.' If a Gene Evidence Narrative is available for this gene, incorporate its mechanistic insight.

claim_type.description: EXTERNAL if the sentence cites a PMID from the provided literature; INFERENCE if no PMID is cited and the claim draws on latent biological knowledge.

statistical_context.description: One sentence summarising the statistical evidence for this theme: include the anchor term FDR, fold enrichment, and gene count. Plain text only — no markdown formatting. Use the numbers directly from the input — do not round or approximate.

hub_genes.description: Explanations for up to 20 hub genes — prioritise those appearing in the most themes. Only include genes that are listed as hub_genes in the input data.

gene.description: Gene symbol. MUST be a gene listed in the input hub_genes section.

narrative.description: 1-2 sentences explaining the MECHANISTIC basis for this gene appearing across multiple themes. Plain text only — no markdown formatting. Do NOT merely list the themes — explain HOW the gene's molecular function connects them. If a Mechanistic Narrative is available for this gene, incorporate its insight. BAD: 'ABC appears in migration and signaling themes.' GOOD: 'ABC coordinates migration and signaling via its dual kinase/scaffold role, phosphorylating downstream targets in both pathways PMID:20027291.'

claim_type.description: EXTERNAL if the narrative cites a PMID from the provided literature; INFERENCE if no PMID is cited and the claim draws on latent biological knowledge.

overall_summary.description: 3-4 paragraph overall biological narrative synthesising all themes into a coherent story. Each element of this array is one paragraph (a plain text string — no markdown formatting). Do not include sub-headings — write continuous prose. The final paragraph should address any limitations or caveats.
